title: 5-HTP (Griffonia) — serotonin precursor, used right
body_html:
5-HTP (5-hydroxytryptophan) is extracted from the seeds of the West African Griffonia simplicifolia and is the direct precursor to serotonin. The validated daily dose for sleep latency and mild low mood is 50–200 mg, taken in the evening with food.

How it works

5-HTP crosses the blood-brain barrier and is converted to serotonin (and downstream, melatonin). It is most consistently studied for sleep latency and mild low mood; benefit on clinical depression is mixed and dose-sensitive.

How to use it

Start at 50 mg in the evening with food. Step up to 100 mg if needed. Most users do not exceed 200 mg/day without supervision. Cycle off after 12 weeks of continuous use.

Who should not take it

Do not combine with SSRIs, SNRIs, MAOIs, tramadol, dextromethorphan or other serotonergic drugs — risk of serotonin syndrome. Speak to your doctor before use if you take any antidepressant.

5-HTP FAQ

Does 5-HTP help me fall asleep?

Most users report reduced sleep latency at 100–200 mg taken 30–60 minutes before bed.

Can I take it with my antidepressant?

No — do not combine with SSRIs, SNRIs or MAOIs without medical supervision. Risk of serotonin syndrome.

How long can I take it?

Cycle off after ~12 weeks of continuous use to allow normal feedback loops to reset.

Will I feel it the first night?

Some users do, but evening calm and sleep benefits typically build over 7–14 days.

Safe in pregnancy?

Avoid — insufficient safety data.

Shop Vivid 5-HTP →

title: About Vivid Health
body_html:
Vivid Health comes from the same family behind One Life. The roots are not corporate. They started with a household looking for better support through natural health, then grew into a retail environment where people could ask practical questions and get human guidance.

Over the years, customers kept bringing the same quiet frustration to the counter: the shelves were full, labels were confusing, and it was hard to know which supplement was actually worth taking every day. Vivid was built from those conversations.

Before Vivid had its own Shopify store, customers were already buying the range through One Life. More than 6,000 Vivid units have sold through that retail environment, which gives the standalone brand a real foundation instead of a cold catalogue launch.

The promise is simple: focused formulations, transparent labels, clear active doses, plant-first choices where they make sense, vegetarian capsules where possible, nationwide delivery, and access to real people when you need help choosing.

title: Ashwagandha (KSM-66) — the cortisol study guide
body_html:
Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera) is the most-studied adaptogen in modern research. The KSM-66 extract — a full-spectrum root, standardised to 5% withanolides — is the form used in the majority of human trials. The trial-validated dose for stress, sleep and cortisol reduction is 300–600 mg of KSM-66 daily for 60+ days.

What the trials actually show

Across >15 randomised controlled trials, KSM-66 at 600 mg/day for 8 weeks reduced morning serum cortisol by 20–30% and improved Perceived Stress Scale scores by 30–44% vs placebo (Chandrasekhar et al., 2012; Salve et al., 2019; Lopresti et al., 2019). Sleep quality, measured by Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index, improves on the same dose at 8 weeks.

Why root extract beats leaf

Most cheap SA ashwagandha is leaf or whole-plant powder. KSM-66 is root-only, standardised to a clinically meaningful 5% withanolide content. It is the form 90%+ of published cortisol research uses. Vivid uses KSM-66 from Ixoreal Biomed (India) — the original patented extract.

How to take it

Start with 300 mg KSM-66 once daily for 7 days; assess. Step up to 600 mg/day (300 mg morning + 300 mg evening, or 600 mg morning) for 8 weeks before judging effect. Take with food. Effects on stress, sleep and recovery are cumulative, not acute.

Who should be careful

Avoid in pregnancy. Speak to your doctor if you have an autoimmune condition (lupus, Hashimoto's), are on thyroid medication or immunosuppressants, or take sedatives. Stop 2 weeks before surgery.

Ashwagandha FAQ

What's the right ashwagandha dose?

300–600 mg of standardised KSM-66 root extract daily for 8 weeks before judging effect. Most SA brands underdose at 100–200 mg of generic leaf powder.

When will I feel it?

Stress and sleep benefits typically appear at 4–8 weeks of consistent daily dosing. It is not an acute, take-and-feel-it supplement.

Is KSM-66 better than Sensoril?

Sensoril is leaf + root extract standardised to 10% withanolides at a lower dose (125 mg). Both have human trials. KSM-66 has more cortisol-specific data in healthy adults.

Can I take it long-term?

Most clinical trials run 60–90 days. Long-term safety data is limited; consider a 2-week break after 12 weeks of continuous use.

Why is it not safe in pregnancy?

Traditional and modern guidance both list ashwagandha as a uterine stimulant. Avoid during pregnancy and while trying to conceive.

Shop Vivid KSM-66 ashwagandha →

title: Ashwagandha for stress and cortisol — the trial-validated dose
body_html:
Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera) is the most studied adaptogen in modern research. The trial data is unusually consistent: at the right dose, it measurably lowers perceived stress and salivary cortisol over 8 weeks. The problem is most supermarket bottles don't use the trial-validated extract.

What "adaptogen" actually means

An adaptogen is a non-toxic compound that helps the body resist physical, chemical, and biological stressors by normalising the HPA (hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal) axis. Russian researchers coined the term in the 1940s. Ashwagandha, rhodiola, and panax ginseng are the three with the strongest modern evidence.

For ashwagandha specifically, the mechanism is partially through GABA-mimetic effects and partially through downregulation of cortisol production over weeks of consistent use.

The KSM-66 question

Most of the high-quality randomised controlled trials on ashwagandha use a specific standardised extract called KSM-66 — root only (no leaves), water-extracted, standardised to 5% withanolides. Other extracts may work, but the trial data is mostly with KSM-66.

Check the label. If a bottle just says "ashwagandha root powder, 500mg" with no standardisation percentage, you don't actually know what's in it.

The dose and the protocol

The trial-validated dose is 300–600mg of standardised extract, twice daily, for at least 8 weeks. The benchmark trial (Chandrasekhar et al., 2012) used 300mg KSM-66 twice daily and reported a 44% reduction in perceived stress scale (PSS) scores and a 28% reduction in morning salivary cortisol vs placebo.

Effects build over weeks — most people notice a difference in sleep and resilience around weeks 3–4, with the cortisol shift showing at weeks 6–8. Stop after 12 weeks for a 2-week break before resuming if you want to continue long-term.

Cautions

  • Pregnancy and breastfeeding: not recommended.
  • Thyroid medication: ashwagandha can raise free T3 and T4. If you're on levothyroxine, check with your doctor first — your dose may need adjustment.
  • Immunosuppressants: ashwagandha modulates immune function. Avoid if you're on cyclosporine, tacrolimus, or similar.
  • Sedatives: additive effects with benzodiazepines or sleep medication.

What Vivid stocks

We don't currently stock a standalone ashwagandha — it's in our adaptogen pipeline for Q3 2026. In the meantime, the closest Vivid match for stress support is Vivid Tranquil (magnesium glycinate + passion flower + valerian + L-theanine). For a true ashwagandha buy today, Onelife stocks several brands at onelife.co.za/collections/conditions-stress.

title: Curcumin — why bioavailability is the only number that matters
body_html:
Curcumin is the bright-orange polyphenol in turmeric responsible for its anti-inflammatory effect. Plain turmeric is famously badly absorbed — ~1%. Modern formulations with piperine, lipid carriers or phytosome technology raise absorption 7–20×. The validated daily dose, in a bioavailable form, is 500–1,000 mg curcuminoids.

Why the carrier is everything

Curcumin alone is hydrophobic, poorly soluble and rapidly metabolised. The clinical effect — joint, mood, cardiometabolic — depends on the carrier. Established forms: BCM-95 (curcumin + turmeric essential oil), Meriva (phytosome), Theracurmin (sub-micron) and C3 Complex + BioPerine (Sabinsa's piperine-paired curcuminoid extract). Vivid uses Sabinsa C3 + BioPerine.

What it's been studied for

Major systematic reviews support curcumin at 500–1,000 mg/day for joint pain (osteoarthritis), mood (depressive symptoms), metabolic markers (CRP, fasting glucose) and post-exercise inflammation (Hewlings & Kalman, 2017).

Curcumin FAQ

Why isn't 'turmeric latte' a real dose?

A teaspoon of turmeric is ~200 mg curcuminoids — and 1% of that is bioavailable. The clinically effective dose is 500 mg+ of curcuminoids in a bioavailability-enhanced form.

Will it interact with blood thinners?

Curcumin has mild antiplatelet effects. Speak to your doctor if on warfarin/clopidogrel or before surgery.

Does it stain teeth?

Less so in capsule form than turmeric latte form. Capsules bypass the mouth entirely.

Vegan?

Yes.

Safe in pregnancy?

Use of curcumin supplementation in pregnancy is not advised; dietary turmeric in food is fine.

Shop Vivid curcumin →

title: Energy & fatigue — supplements that actually move the needle
body_html:
Persistent fatigue almost always reflects a measurable deficiency or sub-clinical issue. The four highest-yield supplements for SA adults: methylated B-complex, CoQ10, iron (women), and vitamin D3. Most over-the-counter "energy" supplements (caffeine + adaptogens) only treat symptoms.

Start with bloodwork

If fatigue is persistent, get baseline labs: ferritin, vitamin D 25(OH)D, B12, thyroid (TSH, free T4). Most SA fatigue is iron- or D-driven and resolves on correction within 8–12 weeks.

B-complex — methylated forms

Use a B-complex with methylfolate (5-MTHF) and methylcobalamin, not folic acid and cyanocobalamin. Up to 40% of South Africans carry an MTHFR variant that limits conversion of synthetic folic acid. Daily B-complex with B6 (P-5-P) 25 mg + B12 (methyl) 1,000 mcg + folate 400 mcg covers most.

CoQ10 — particularly on statins

Statin therapy depletes CoQ10. If you take a statin and feel fatigued/sore, 100–200 mg ubiquinol or ubiquinone daily is the most-cited adjunct.

Energy FAQ

Is caffeine bad for energy?

Caffeine masks fatigue; it doesn't fix it. Pair with L-theanine and address the underlying drivers (iron, D, B12, sleep).

Will B12 give me an immediate lift?

Only if you were deficient. In sufficient adults, supplemental B12 doesn't acutely boost energy.

Iron — should I take it?

Only if your ferritin is <70 (women) or <100 (athletes). Iron supplementation in sufficient adults causes oxidative stress and constipation.

What about adaptogens?

Ashwagandha and rhodiola modestly improve subjective energy in stressed adults. They work on stress, not metabolism.

Why is methylated B-complex better?

If you carry an MTHFR variant (40%+ of SA adults), synthetic folic acid is poorly converted. Methylated forms are bioactive directly.

Shop Vivid energy range →

title: Free naturopath consultation
body_html:
Free 15-minute consultation with a Vivid naturopath

Not sure where to start? Book a free 15-minute call with one of our in-house naturopaths. We'll listen, ask a few questions, and recommend a starting formulation that fits your goal, your budget, and what you're already taking.

What you'll get

  • A 15-minute call (or WhatsApp message if you prefer) with a qualified naturopath
  • A short shortlist of 2–3 Vivid formulations matched to your goal
  • Dosage guidance and any interaction warnings if you're on prescription medication
  • No upsell pressure — if our formulations aren't right for you, we'll say so

What we don't do

We don't diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent disease. If you have a medical condition, are pregnant or breastfeeding, or take prescription medication, we'll always recommend you check with your doctor or pharmacist first. The consultation is for educational purposes only.

How it works

  1. Email info@vividhealthsa.co.za or WhatsApp +27 71 374 4910 with your goal in one sentence (e.g. "sleeping through the night", "post-COVID energy", "joint pain after running")
  2. We'll reply within one business day with two or three time slots
  3. 15-minute call. Most people make a decision on the call

WhatsApp us to book   Email instead

Vivid Health consultations are provided by qualified South African naturopaths and are intended for educational purposes only. They do not substitute for medical advice from your doctor or pharmacist.

Can I return an opened supplement?

title: Immunity — what the SA winter and Highveld pollen demand
body_html:
SA immunity has two seasonal pressures: winter respiratory infections (May–August) and Highveld grass pollen (September–November). The evidence-led baseline stack for both: vitamin D3, zinc picolinate, quercetin and a vitamin C complex.

The winter stack

Vitamin D3 2,000–4,000 IU + zinc picolinate 15–30 mg + buffered vitamin C 500–1,000 mg, daily through autumn and winter (March–August). D3 is the single most-studied modulator of respiratory infection susceptibility.

The hay-fever stack (Sept–Nov on the Highveld)

Quercetin 500 mg + bromelain 100 mg + vitamin C 500 mg, twice daily, starting 2–4 weeks before peak season. Stabilises mast cells upstream of histamine release.

If you're already symptomatic

Acute support: zinc 30–50 mg/day for up to 7 days, elderberry syrup 15 mL twice daily for 5 days, vitamin C 1,000 mg three times daily. Speak to your pharmacist for prescription options if symptoms persist beyond 5 days.

Immunity FAQ

Is vitamin C still relevant?

Yes — at 500–1,000 mg daily it modestly reduces respiratory-infection duration. Higher doses are useful acutely.

Zinc picolinate or gluconate?

Picolinate has slightly better absorption. Both are clinically used. Avoid oxide — poorly absorbed.

Does elderberry actually work?

Cochrane-level evidence is modest but consistent for symptom-duration reduction in URI when taken early.

Can I take this stack daily year-round?

D3 and C, yes. Zinc — pause to 15 mg/day after 8 weeks of higher dosing to avoid copper depletion.

Safe in pregnancy?

D3 and C generally yes. Zinc up to 11 mg. Quercetin and elderberry — consult your healthcare provider.

Shop Vivid immunity range →

title: Ingredient standards
body_html:
Vivid Health formulas are built around clear active doses, plant-first actives, vegetarian capsules, and specific outcome-led formulation.

What we avoid

We avoid vague proprietary blends and hidden active doses. Where an ingredient is included for a specific purpose, the label should make the amount clear.

How to use the range

Choose the formulation that matches the system you want to support. Read the product label, follow the suggested use, and speak to a qualified healthcare professional if you have a medical condition or use chronic medication.

title: L-theanine — calm focus without the crash
body_html:
L-theanine is an amino acid found naturally in green tea. It promotes a relaxed-but-alert state by increasing alpha brain-wave activity, and famously smooths the spike-and-crash of caffeine. The validated dose is 100–200 mg, ideally as the patented Suntheanine®.

What L-theanine does

L-theanine raises GABA, serotonin and dopamine while increasing alpha-wave EEG activity associated with calm, focused attention (Nobre et al., 2008). When combined with caffeine, it improves accuracy and attention-switching while reducing jitter and post-caffeine fatigue (Owen et al., 2008).

The caffeine pairing

A 2:1 ratio of L-theanine to caffeine (e.g. 200 mg L-theanine + 100 mg caffeine) is the most replicated combination in cognitive trials. For SA users on a 200 mg-caffeine morning routine, pair with 200–400 mg L-theanine.

Why Suntheanine®

L-theanine has two enantiomers (L and D). Only the L-form is bioactive. Cheap supplements often supply a racemic mix, halving the effective dose. Suntheanine® is enzymatically produced 99%+ pure L-theanine, used in the majority of published trials. Vivid uses Suntheanine.

L-theanine FAQ

Is L-theanine a sedative?

No — it produces a calm, alert state, not drowsiness. Most users can take it during the workday without sleepiness.

Can I take it with coffee?

Yes, and that's the most-studied pairing. A 2:1 L-theanine:caffeine ratio reduces caffeine jitter and improves attention.

What's the right dose?

100–200 mg for daytime calm focus; 200–400 mg paired with caffeine; 400 mg in the evening for wind-down.

Does it interact with medication?

Speak to your doctor if you take blood-pressure medication (mild additive lowering) or sedatives.

Is it safe in pregnancy?

Limited data — consult your healthcare provider.

Shop Vivid L-theanine →

title: Magnesium for sleep — which form, what dose, when to take it
body_html:
If you've been told "magnesium helps you sleep" and stocked up on a generic supermarket bottle, you've probably noticed it doesn't do much. The form matters. The dose matters. The timing matters. Here's what the trial data actually says.

Why magnesium helps sleep at all

Magnesium is a cofactor in over 300 enzyme reactions, but the sleep connection runs through three specific mechanisms: it binds to GABA receptors (the same family of receptors benzodiazepines target, but gently), it regulates melatonin synthesis, and it reduces nighttime cortisol. Deficient adults — and roughly 50% of South Africans don't hit the 320–420mg RDA from food — tend to wake more often, sleep more lightly, and report less restorative sleep.

The form question — glycinate vs citrate vs oxide

Magnesium glycinate is the sleep form. The glycine carrier itself is calming, the magnesium absorbs at around 80% bioavailability, and there's no GI distress. Studies in older adults at 320mg/night showed significant improvements in sleep onset latency, total sleep time, and serum melatonin.

Magnesium citrate is a daily form for general supplementation but also a mild laxative — fine for daytime, bad for sleep if you take it too close to bed.

Magnesium oxide is what's in cheap supermarket multivitamins. 4% absorbed. Skip it.

Magnesium threonate is sometimes marketed as crossing the blood-brain barrier better — the evidence is preliminary and mostly from mouse studies. Doesn't justify the 5x price tag yet.

How much, when

The clinical sleep dose is 200–400mg of elemental magnesium, taken 60–90 minutes before bed. Lower end if you're under 60kg or magnesium-sensitive; higher end if you're heavier or doing intense exercise (which depletes magnesium in sweat).

Take it with a small amount of food if you find it uncomfortable on an empty stomach, but it's not strictly necessary.

What we put in Vivid Tranquil

Vivid Tranquil is our calm-and-sleep formulation. It pairs 300mg of magnesium glycinate with passion flower, valerian, and L-theanine — the four ingredients with the strongest sleep trial data, at doses that match the studies. Not a sledgehammer. Calm without sedation.

View Tranquil →

When magnesium alone isn't enough

If you've been taking 300mg of glycinate for 4 weeks and your sleep hasn't shifted, the issue probably isn't magnesium deficiency. Common culprits: undiagnosed sleep apnoea, elevated evening cortisol, alcohol within 3 hours of bed, screen exposure past 21:00, or a too-warm bedroom (>19°C). A naturopath consultation can help triage — book a free 15-minute call.

title: Magnesium glycinate — the most absorbable form, explained
body_html:
Magnesium glycinate is magnesium bonded to the amino acid glycine. It absorbs ~25% better than magnesium oxide, doesn't loosen the stool the way citrate often does, and is the form most clinically used for sleep, stress, and muscle recovery. The trial-validated daily dose is 200–400 mg of elemental magnesium, taken in the evening.

Why magnesium glycinate beats oxide and citrate

Most cheap SA multivitamins list "400 mg magnesium oxide" on the label. Magnesium oxide is roughly 4% absorbed — meaning a 400 mg dose delivers about 16 mg of elemental magnesium to the bloodstream. Magnesium glycinate runs at 25–30% bioavailability with a far gentler gastrointestinal profile (Walker et al., 2003; Schuchardt & Hahn, 2017). For SA buyers, the practical takeaway is: check the form, not just the mg.

How magnesium glycinate compares

Form Approx. absorption Best for GI effect
Glycinate ~25–30% Sleep, stress, recovery Gentle
Citrate ~16% Constipation, basic supplementation Can loosen stool
Oxide ~4% Cheap multivitamins (not recommended) Often diarrhoea
L-threonate ~10% Cognition (crosses BBB) Gentle

What dose actually works

Clinical trials on magnesium for sleep, anxiety and muscle cramping consistently use 200–400 mg elemental daily, 30–60 minutes before bed. South African soils are magnesium-deficient and most adult diets fall short of the RDA. Vivid's bottle shows the elemental milligrams — not the salt weight — so you can dose accurately.

Who should be careful

Speak to your pharmacist if you take potassium-sparing diuretics, certain antibiotics (tetracyclines, quinolones — separate by 2 hrs), bisphosphonates, or have severe kidney impairment. Pregnant and breastfeeding women should consult their healthcare provider before starting.

Magnesium glycinate FAQ

Can I take magnesium glycinate every day?

Yes — at 200–400 mg elemental daily, magnesium glycinate is well-tolerated long term and addresses the dietary shortfall most SA adults run.

When should I take it?

Take it 30–60 minutes before bed for sleep and stress benefits. Split across the day if you're using it for muscle cramping.

Will it interact with my medication?

Separate from tetracycline and quinolone antibiotics by 2 hours. Speak to your pharmacist if you take potassium-sparing diuretics, bisphosphonates, or have kidney impairment.

Is it safe in pregnancy?

Consult your healthcare provider. Magnesium glycinate is often used during pregnancy under supervision for cramping and sleep but should be cleared with your GP or OB.

How is Vivid's magnesium glycinate different?

We list the elemental milligrams on the label — not the salt weight — so the number you see is the number that lands in your system. Every batch is independently tested. Sourced from Albion/Balchem, Germany.

Shop Vivid magnesium glycinate →

title: Omega-3 (EPA + DHA) — the dose that actually matters
body_html:
Omega-3 EPA and DHA are the actives that drive every benefit (cardiovascular, cognitive, mood, joint). The label number that matters is not "fish oil 1,000 mg" — it is the combined EPA+DHA per serving. The validated daily dose for general health is 1,000–2,000 mg combined EPA+DHA.

Why most SA fish oil underdoses

A typical "1,000 mg fish oil" SA softgel delivers 180 mg EPA + 120 mg DHA — 300 mg of actives. The validated cardiovascular and cognitive dose is 1,000 mg+ EPA+DHA, which means 3–6 of those softgels. Vivid lists the EPA and DHA milligrams on the front of the bottle.

Triglyceride vs ethyl ester form

Triglyceride-form omega-3 absorbs ~70% better than ethyl-ester form (Dyerberg et al., 2010). Re-esterified triglyceride (rTG) is the highest-bioavailability form. Vivid uses rTG from GC Rieber Oils (Norway/Peru) with IFOS 5-star certification for purity and freshness.

Who needs what

Goal Combined EPA+DHA
Cardiovascular maintenance 1,000 mg/day
Brain & mood 1,500–2,000 mg/day, EPA-leaning
Triglyceride lowering 2,000–4,000 mg/day (clinical)
Pregnancy / breastfeeding 200–400 mg DHA/day

Omega-3 FAQ

EPA or DHA — which matters?

Both. EPA dominates anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular benefits; DHA is the structural fatty acid in brain and retina.

Fish oil or krill?

Krill carries phospholipid-bound omega-3 with astaxanthin — premium but expensive. For most users, IFOS-certified triglyceride fish oil at a real dose is more cost-effective.

Does it interact with blood thinners?

Speak to your doctor if you take warfarin, aspirin or have a clotting condition.

When should I take it?

With food, ideally a fat-containing meal. Split across the day if you're taking 3+ softgels.

Why does Vivid's omega-3 have a freshness date?

Oxidised omega-3 is worse than no omega-3. We list batch peroxide values and a clear expiry.

Shop Vivid omega-3 →

title: Omega-3 for brain health — EPA, DHA, and the real dose
body_html:
Roughly 25% of your brain's dry weight is DHA — a long-chain omega-3 fatty acid. You can't make it efficiently from plants. The standard South African diet provides about 50mg/day. The brain-supportive dose in randomised trials is 20 times that. Most supplement bottles don't get you there.

EPA vs DHA — which is for the brain

DHA (docosahexaenoic acid) is the structural omega-3. It sits in neuronal cell membranes, keeps them fluid, and is essential for synaptic function. Low DHA correlates with faster cognitive decline in observational studies.

EPA (eicosapentaenoic acid) is the anti-inflammatory omega-3. It modulates inflammatory cytokines, which matters for mood (depression has a strong inflammatory component) and cardiovascular health.

Both come from cold-water fish. The body interconverts them at a low rate, so you need both in the bottle.

How much, what ratio

The standard cognitive-support dose in trials is 1000mg combined EPA+DHA per day, ideally with a 2:1 EPA:DHA ratio for general use or 1:2 if you're targeting brain over heart. Higher doses (2000–4000mg) are used for major depression and post-cardiac-event prevention.

Check the back of any fish oil bottle. A "1000mg fish oil capsule" often contains only 180mg EPA + 120mg DHA — the other 700mg is filler oil. You'd need 3–4 capsules to hit the trial dose.

Why plant omegas aren't enough

Flaxseed and chia provide ALA (alpha-linolenic acid), which the body can convert to EPA at about 5% efficiency and DHA at under 1%. To hit 1000mg of EPA/DHA from flax alone you'd need 20+ tablespoons a day. Not realistic. For brain support, you need marine omega-3.

What we put in Vivid Omega Oil

Vivid Omega Oil is wild-caught, third-party tested for heavy metals, and delivers 660mg EPA + 440mg DHA per 2-capsule serving — 1100mg combined, right in the trial range. Stored cold, encapsulated under nitrogen to prevent oxidation.

View Omega Oil →

When to take it

With food, ideally containing some fat — omega-3 is fat-soluble. Splitting the dose (morning + evening) gives steadier blood levels. Effects on cognition show in 8–12 weeks of consistent use, not overnight.

title: Our approach
body_html:
Our entire approach can be summarised in three sentences. We tell you the milligrams. We use plants first. We build each formulation for a single specific job. Here is what each one means in practice.

1. We tell you the milligrams

Every active ingredient is named with the exact dose, on the front of every bottle. No proprietary blends. No "complex matrices." No "natural extract — adequate amount" hand-waving. If the clinical trial used 810mg of MSM, our MSM has 810mg of MSM. If the saw palmetto research required a 4:1 standardised extract at 320mg, that is what is in the bottle.

This sounds simple. The reason most supplement brands do not do it is that proprietary blends let manufacturers under-dose expensive actives behind a single combined milligram figure. A 1000mg "Joint Complex" might contain 950mg cheap filler and 50mg of the active that actually does the work. We refuse to participate in that.

Practical commitment: every Vivid product page shows the active milligrams. Every Vivid label shows the active milligrams. Both numbers match. Every batch is tested by an independent third-party laboratory and the certificate of analysis is available on request.

2. We use plants first

Where a plant extract has the evidence, we use it. Where it does not, we use the standardised active. We do not use whole-plant powders out of romanticism, and we do not use synthetic isolates because they are cheaper. We use whatever has the strongest clinical evidence for the specific outcome.

The decision tree for every Vivid formulation:

  1. Is there a high-quality randomised controlled trial on this ingredient for this outcome? If no, we do not include it.
  2. What form did the trial use? Standardised extract, whole-plant powder, isolated active, food-source nutrient. We use the form the evidence supports.
  3. What was the trial dose? We match it. Not a token amount. Not an "effective range" hedge.
  4. Vegetable capsules where applicable. Sugar-free. No artificial colourants. South African manufactured in a SAHPRA-aligned facility.

This is why Vivid Tranquil pairs magnesium glycinate (the form with the strongest sleep evidence) with passion flower and valerian (the herbs with the strongest sleep evidence) at the trial doses — and not with melatonin, despite melatonin being the most popular ingredient in the category.

3. We build for a single specific job

Each Vivid formulation does one thing. No "everything wellness" stacks. No "mens daily multivitamin" with 47 ingredients at sub-therapeutic doses. If you take a Vivid bottle, you know precisely what outcome you are working on.

This is the opposite of the supermarket multivitamin model, which tries to be everything to everyone by including small amounts of many ingredients. The problem with that model is that the doses are too low to do anything for any one outcome, and there is no way to titrate the ingredients you actually need.

Our 52 formulations cover eight outcome categories: Gut Health, Immunity, Energy & Vitality, Stress/Sleep & Mood, Joints & Mobility, Womens Health, Mens Health, and Daily Nutrients. Pick the outcome you are working on. Pick the formulation built for it. Take it for 6–8 weeks. Assess. Adjust.

Where we manufacture

Vivid Health products are manufactured in Centurion, South Africa, in a SAHPRA-aligned facility that has been producing supplements for the Onelife Health retail chain since 1996. The facility is GMP-aligned, third-party audited, and processes only oral solid-dose supplement formulations (capsules and powders) — not combined with topical skincare, not co-manufactured with veterinary products. This matters because contamination risk is lowest in dedicated facilities.

Every batch is independently tested for identity (is it actually what we said it is), potency (does it actually contain the milligrams we claim), and heavy metals (lead, arsenic, cadmium, mercury). Pass results are required before release. Fail results are quarantined and reprocessed or discarded.

Where we source from

Plant material is sourced primarily from Southern African producers where the local climate matches the plants native conditions — including Cape Aloe Ferox, Devils Claw from Namibia, Honeybush and Rooibos. Imported actives where unavoidable (omega-3 from cold-water marine sources, ashwagandha from India, valerian from the Western Cape but originally European) are sourced from suppliers with documented quality control and confirmed organic or wild-harvest certification.

We do not buy bulk powders from third-party traders who cannot trace their material. This means our cost of goods is higher than competitors who do, and we choose to absorb that cost rather than compromise on traceability.

Who designed these formulations

Vivid formulations are designed by the Onelife Health naturopath team — qualified South African practitioners who have been advising customers in our Centurion, Glen Village, and Edenvale retail stores for decades. The decision criteria for every formulation is: what would we recommend to a customer who walked into the store with this specific goal?

That practitioner-grounded approach is why our Stress, Sleep & Mood formulation is called Tranquil (and not "Mental Health Plus Complex"), and why our joint formulation is called Flexijoint Advanced (not "Joints & Mobility Daily Synergy Matrix"). The language matches how a real person would describe what the product is for.

What the dose-stated promise costs us

Being radically transparent has trade-offs:

  • Our customers can comparison-shop on dose per rand — we welcome this
  • Our cost of goods is higher than blend-based competitors
  • We cannot make vague "supports immune function" claims at low doses to fill catalogue range — every Vivid product must justify its dose with evidence
  • Some ingredients we would love to formulate around (e.g. NMN, glutathione) are not yet supported by strong enough clinical evidence at affordable doses — so we wait

These trade-offs are why most supplement brands do not do this. They are also why the customers who do their homework choose Vivid.

Questions a careful customer should ask any supplement brand

  1. What is the dose of each active ingredient? (If they will not tell you, walk away.)
  2. What form of the active is used? (Magnesium oxide is not magnesium glycinate.)
  3. Is the dose in the bottle the same as the dose used in the clinical trial?
  4. Where is it manufactured?
  5. Is the batch tested by an independent laboratory? Can I see the certificate?
  6. Are there any actives that are not on the front-of-label?

We can answer yes to all six on every Vivid product. Most brands cannot answer yes to half. We think you deserve to know which is which.

Want a real human to walk you through this?

Our in-house naturopaths offer free 15-minute consultations. They will explain the dose, the form, the form factor, and which Vivid formulation fits the goal you are working on. No upsell pressure — if our formulation is not right for you, they will say so.

Book a free 15-minute consultation

title: Quercetin — the Highveld hay-fever flavonoid
body_html:
Quercetin is a plant flavonoid found in onions, apples and capers that stabilises mast cells — the immune cells that release histamine. It is the most-studied natural option for hay-fever and seasonal allergy support. The validated daily dose is 500–1,000 mg, typically paired with bromelain to improve absorption.

Why Gauteng needs it from September

The Highveld hay-fever season (late August to early November) is driven by grass pollen, with rye, kikuyu and Bermuda grass dominant. Quercetin + bromelain stacked daily 2–4 weeks before peak season has the strongest anecdotal track record for symptom prevention.

Pair with vitamin C and bromelain

Vitamin C extends quercetin's plasma half-life; bromelain (a pineapple enzyme) modestly improves its absorption. A common stack is quercetin 500 mg + bromelain 100 mg + vitamin C 500 mg, twice daily.

Quercetin FAQ

When should I start taking it for hay fever?

Two to four weeks before pollen-season peak — late July through August in Gauteng — gives the mast-cell stabilising effect time to build.

Can I take it with antihistamines?

Generally yes — quercetin works upstream of histamine release, not on the receptor. Speak to your pharmacist if you are on prescription medications.

Does it interact with medication?

Quercetin can affect cytochrome P450 enzymes. Speak to your doctor if you take blood-thinners, calcium-channel blockers or chemotherapy agents.

Vegan?

Yes.

Safe in pregnancy?

Insufficient safety data — consult your healthcare provider.

Shop Vivid quercetin →

title: Shipping and returns
body_html:
Standard delivery is free for orders of R400 or more. Orders below R400 are charged the delivery fee shown at checkout.

Delivery timing

Orders are normally packed within 1 to 2 business days after payment clears. Major centres are usually delivered within 2 to 5 business days after dispatch, while regional areas can take longer.

Returns

Unopened, unused, sealed products can be returned within 30 days of delivery. Opened supplements cannot usually be returned for change of mind because they are ingestible health products. Damaged, incorrect, or defective items should be reported within 48 hours.

title: Sleep — the supplement guide for South Africans
body_html:
The four ingredients with the strongest evidence for sleep are magnesium glycinate, L-theanine, 5-HTP and ashwagandha (KSM-66). They work through different mechanisms — minerals, GABA, serotonin precursor, cortisol — and stack well together. Below: who benefits from what, doses, and timing.

The evening stack for most SA adults

The most replicated daily evening sleep stack: magnesium glycinate 300 mg + L-theanine 200 mg + ashwagandha KSM-66 300 mg taken 30–60 minutes before bed. Each ingredient is well-tolerated, no interaction with each other, and effects build over 2–4 weeks.

If sleep latency is the problem

Add 5-HTP 100 mg in the evening for shorter time-to-sleep. Do not combine with SSRIs/SNRIs. Cycle off after 12 weeks.

If you wake at 3am

Early-morning wake is classic cortisol-driven. Prioritise ashwagandha KSM-66 600 mg/day for 8 weeks + magnesium glycinate at night. Address evening alcohol and caffeine timing.

Why this stack, not melatonin first

Exogenous melatonin works for shifted circadian rhythms (jet lag, shift work) but most SA insomnia is stress- and mineral-driven. Magnesium/theanine/ashwagandha treat the upstream cause; melatonin can be added if the rhythm itself is off (300 mcg–1 mg, not the over-dosed 3–10 mg most SA shops sell).

Sleep FAQ

What's the single most effective sleep supplement?

Magnesium glycinate at 300 mg elemental in the evening has the broadest evidence base and the lowest interaction profile. Start there.

How long before I see results?

Most users notice within 7–14 days. Full effect of the ashwagandha component takes 6–8 weeks.

Are these safe with sleep meds?

Magnesium and theanine are generally safe alongside common SA prescription sleep medications, but always check with your pharmacist.

Can I take all four together?

Yes — they work on different pathways. Many SA adults do this nightly as the 'evening stack'.

What about CBD?

Mixed evidence in randomised trials, and quality control in SA is highly variable. Start with the evidence-led stack above; consider CBD only if persistent issues.

Shop Vivid sleep range →

title: Stress & cortisol — what the trials actually support
body_html:
Three ingredients carry the strongest randomised-controlled-trial evidence for stress and cortisol reduction in healthy adults: ashwagandha (KSM-66), magnesium glycinate and L-theanine. Used together they cover the major stress pathways — HPA axis, neuromuscular tension, alpha-wave attention — without interaction risk.

The daily stress stack

Ashwagandha KSM-66 600 mg + magnesium glycinate 300 mg + L-theanine 200 mg, daily for 8 weeks. Split ashwagandha across morning + evening; L-theanine in the morning with coffee; magnesium in the evening.

What the trials show

KSM-66 at 600 mg/day reduces morning cortisol 20–30% and Perceived Stress Scale scores 30–44% at 8 weeks vs placebo (Chandrasekhar 2012, Salve 2019, Lopresti 2019). Magnesium supplementation improves subjective stress and sleep in mineral-deficient adults — and most SA diets are mineral-deficient (FAO).

If anxiety is the symptom

Increase L-theanine to 400 mg/day split AM and PM. Speak to your doctor if you take SSRIs, benzodiazepines or beta-blockers.

Stress FAQ

What's the single most-supported stress supplement?

Ashwagandha KSM-66 at 600 mg/day for 8 weeks. The cortisol data is the strongest in this category.

Does L-theanine work without caffeine?

Yes — it produces calm alertness on its own. The caffeine pairing is just particularly well-studied.

Can I take this stack with antidepressants?

Generally yes, but check with your prescribing doctor — particularly for ashwagandha + SSRIs and L-theanine + sedatives.

How long until I feel it?

Magnesium and L-theanine: days. Ashwagandha: 4–8 weeks for full cortisol effect.

Safe in pregnancy?

Magnesium and L-theanine generally yes under supervision. Ashwagandha: avoid in pregnancy.

Shop Vivid stress range →

title: Vitamin D3 for immunity — South African doses and what the trials actually show
body_html:
South Africa has more sunshine than almost anywhere on earth. South Africans are also, paradoxically, widely vitamin D deficient. A 2019 University of Cape Town study found 40% of adults below the sufficiency threshold. Here's why and what to do about it.

Why South Africans are D-deficient despite the sun

Three reasons:

  • Melanin reduces D3 synthesis. Darker skin needs roughly 3–5 times more sun exposure to make the same amount of D3 as lighter skin. The same 15-minute lunchtime walk that gives a fair-skinned person 1000 IU might give a darker-skinned person 200.
  • Modern indoor lifestyles. Office work, cars, and shopping malls mean even people who think they're "outdoorsy" often get under 30 minutes of bare-skin sun on a normal weekday.
  • Sunscreen. SPF 30+ blocks 95%+ of UVB, which is exactly what your skin uses to make D3.

Why D3 matters for immunity

Vitamin D receptors are present on almost every immune cell — T cells, B cells, macrophages, dendritic cells. It modulates both innate immunity (the first-response system that handles bacteria and viruses) and adaptive immunity (the antibody-producing system). Low serum D correlates with higher risk of upper respiratory infections; supplementation reduced infection risk by 11% on average across 25 randomised trials (Martineau et al., BMJ 2017), with the benefit concentrated in those who were deficient.

D3 also matters for calcium absorption, bone density, mood (especially seasonal affective patterns), and possibly cardiovascular health.

The dose

For maintenance and immune support: 2000 IU/day for adults with adequate baseline status.

For correcting deficiency: 4000–5000 IU/day for 8–12 weeks, then drop to 2000 IU maintenance. Get a 25(OH)D blood test before starting if you can — target serum level is 75–125 nmol/L (30–50 ng/mL).

Higher doses (10,000 IU+) are sometimes used short-term under medical supervision but aren't appropriate for self-supplementation.

D3 vs D2

Always D3 (cholecalciferol), not D2 (ergocalciferol). D3 raises serum levels 50–80% more efficiently and is what your skin produces naturally. D2 is sometimes used in prescription doses because it's cheaper to manufacture, but for daily supplementation D3 is the form to look for on the label.

Take with a meal containing fat — D3 is fat-soluble. Pair with vitamin K2 (which directs the calcium D3 helps absorb into bones rather than soft tissue) at 90–180 mcg of MK-7 form.

What Vivid stocks

Vivid currently distributes D3 within our Immune Plus and broader nutrient range — see Daily Nutrients or Immunity for current formulations. A standalone Vivid D3 + K2 capsule is in the Q3 2026 pipeline.

Practical protocol

  1. If you can, get a 25(OH)D blood test from your GP or any pathology lab (cost in SA: R200–R400).
  2. If under 50 nmol/L: load with 4000 IU/day for 12 weeks, then drop to 2000 IU.
  3. If 50–75 nmol/L: start at 2000 IU/day, retest in 6 months.
  4. If over 75: 1000–2000 IU/day for maintenance is enough.

Aim to retest annually — D3 is well-tolerated but excessive long-term doses (over 10,000 IU/day) can cause hypercalcaemia.

title: Vitamin D3 — the SA dose, why blood levels matter
body_html:
Despite SA's reputation as the sunshine country, indoor lifestyles, melanin and sunscreen mean most South Africans run vitamin D insufficient (25(OH)D < 75 nmol/L). The maintenance dose for adults is 1,000–4,000 IU of D3 (cholecalciferol) daily, ideally to a measured target of 75–150 nmol/L.

Why D3, not D2

D3 (cholecalciferol) raises and maintains serum 25(OH)D roughly 2× more effectively than D2 (ergocalciferol) (Tripkovic et al., 2012). All SA serious supplementation should use D3.

What dose for what level

Goal Daily dose Expected serum 25(OH)D
Maintenance, sufficient 1,000–2,000 IU 75–100 nmol/L
Correction, mild insufficiency 2,000–4,000 IU 100–125 nmol/L
Deficiency (<50 nmol/L) 5,000 IU under supervision retest at 12 weeks

Pair with K2 (MK-7)

Vitamin K2 directs the calcium that D3 helps you absorb to bone and away from soft tissue. A common stack is D3 5,000 IU + K2 (MK-7) 100 mcg daily.

Vitamin D3 FAQ

Can I just get D from sun?

Possibly — but SA epidemiology says most don't. Get a 25(OH)D blood test before assuming you're sufficient.

How much is too much?

The tolerable upper intake is 4,000 IU/day for adults. Higher doses under medical supervision are sometimes used for short-term correction.

When should I take it?

With a fatty meal — D3 is fat-soluble. Morning or evening is equally fine.

Does it interact with medication?

Speak to your doctor on certain anticonvulsants, steroids and weight-loss drugs that affect D3 absorption.

Is it safe in pregnancy?

Yes — sufficiency is recommended in pregnancy. Most prenatal guidelines target 1,000–2,000 IU daily.

Shop Vivid vitamin D3 →

title: Vivid Members — your daily stack, on autopilot
body_html:
A membership for the people who actually take their supplements. R0 to join. Subscribe to any formulation and you're in.

What you get

10%

Off every delivery

Subscribe and save 10% on every order, every cycle, for as long as you stay. Real native Shopify subscriptions — not a coupon code that expires.

∞

Skip, swap, cancel

Manage everything from your customer account in two clicks. Skip a month, swap a bottle, pause, cancel — no email, no waiting, no friction.

R300

Free shipping at R300

Member free-shipping threshold drops from R400 to R300. Makes a real difference if your stack is two or three smaller bottles.

1st

Early access to new formulations

Members get the next-batch drop and any new formulations before they go on the open store. We launch slowly and in small batches — this is how you don't miss it.

How the subscription works

  1. Pick the formulation. On any product page, tap "Subscribe & Save" and choose your delivery cadence — every month or every two months.
  2. Check out as normal. Your card is charged today; the next delivery is automatic.
  3. Manage it from your account. Login → Subscriptions → skip, swap, pause or cancel any time.
  4. That's it. No app, no separate login, no membership card. The discount, the free shipping, and the early access just happen.

Frequently asked

How do I cancel?

Login to your account → Subscriptions → Cancel. It takes about 10 seconds. No phone call, no email, no retention pitch.

What if I'm going away for a month?

Skip the next delivery from your account. Or pause indefinitely — the subscription stays open and you can resume any time.

Can I change frequency mid-subscription?

Yes. Login and switch between monthly and every-two-months at any point. The 10% discount stays the same.

What about the bundles?

Bundles work on subscriptions too. The bundle price is already a discount — subscription stacks an additional 10% on top.

Is there a minimum commitment?

No. Cancel after the first delivery if you want. The whole point of membership is that you're staying because the formulation works, not because we trapped you.

How do the early-access drops work?

When we have a new formulation ready, members get a private link 7 days before public launch. We typically launch in small batches (200–500 bottles) — the early-access window is usually how all of them go.

The simplest way in: take the 2-minute quiz — it maps a personal stack, and the quiz output already toggles subscribe-and-save by default. Or browse all formulations and add a subscription manually.

Take the 2-minute quiz

title: Vivid vs the alternatives
body_html:
Most South African supplement shelves are filled with two kinds of products: imported brands that were bulk-bought, repackaged, and marked up; and large-cap house brands that hide their doses inside proprietary blends. Vivid is neither. Here is the side-by-side.

Vivid vs imported supplements

What you get Imported supplement Vivid Health
Dose on the label Sometimes. Often hidden in a "proprietary blend" Always. Every milligram, named, on the front
Made where USA / EU / India / China — then bulk imported Centurion, South Africa
SA support if you have questions Email a foreign HQ, response in 2–5 days WhatsApp our naturopaths. Same-day reply
Delivery to your door Often via large e-commerce — 3-5 working days Free over R400. 1–3 working days nationwide
Returns / refunds Often via the importer. Long process. 30 days, no questions asked. Direct refund.
SA Rand pricing FX exposure. Prices jump with the rand ZAR-stable. We absorb FX
Formulation expertise on call Customer service rep, not a practitioner 15-min free call with a SA-qualified naturopath

Vivid vs the supermarket multivitamin

Most chemist and supermarket multivitamins use a wide-ingredient, low-dose model — many vitamins and minerals at small amounts in one capsule. The trade-off:

  • You get convenience (one bottle)
  • You lose dose-specificity (most actives are too low to do anything therapeutic)
  • You lose form-specificity (magnesium oxide instead of magnesium glycinate, etc.)
  • You cannot adjust upward when you need more of a specific nutrient

Vivid’s approach is: identify the one or two specific outcomes you are working on, take a single-focus formulation at the trial dose for 6–8 weeks, then reassess. You will spend more on each bottle, but you will use what you buy.

Vivid vs proprietary-blend supplements

A "proprietary blend" is when a label tells you the combined total weight of multiple actives — e.g. "Adrenal Support Blend 800mg" — without telling you the milligrams of each. This is legal but tells you nothing useful. The brand could be using 750mg of cheap rice bran and 50mg of the active that does the work.

Vivid never uses proprietary blends. If a Vivid label says it has Withania Somnifera, it tells you 300mg of Withania Somnifera root extract standardised to 5% withanolides. Every time.

Want to compare a specific product?

Send us the label of whatever you are taking now. We will write back with the Vivid alternative if it exists, or honestly tell you if your current product is fine and you don’t need to switch.

WhatsApp a comparison →
